message:
fix(channel,stream,sync): relax Send/Sync bounds, fix mutex cancel wakeup, swap_remove

Channel (mpsc.rs, session.rs):
  Removed redundant explicit type annotations on reserve() results.
  The compiler infers these correctly and the annotations were stale
  after prior refactors (they referenced concrete types that are now
  more generic).

Watch channel (watch.rs):
  - Renamed SendError::Closed doc from "All receivers dropped" to
    "The sender has been dropped or closed" — the watch channel does
    not close on receiver drop (it's a broadcast cell).
  - Removed empty "# Errors" doc sections from send() and send_modify()
    that were left over from a previous API where these could fail.

Select combinator (select.rs):
  Changed Vec::remove(idx) to Vec::swap_remove(idx) in SelectAllDrain.
  After the winner is extracted, the remaining futures' ordering doesn't
  matter (they're all being drained), so swap_remove's O(1) is preferred
  over remove's O(n) shift.

BroadcastStream (broadcast_stream.rs):
  Relaxed the Stream impl bound from `T: Clone + Send` to `T: Clone`.
  BroadcastStream is a local adapter wrapping a broadcast::Receiver —
  there's no cross-thread requirement at this layer. Test added using
  Rc<String> (non-Send) to verify the relaxed bound compiles and works.

WatchStream (watch_stream.rs):
  Relaxed the Stream impl bound from `T: Clone + Send + Sync` to
  `T: Clone`. Same rationale as BroadcastStream. Test added using
  Rc<String> (non-Send, non-Sync).

Mutex (mutex.rs):
  Fixed cancel-wakeup logic in LockFuture: when a lock attempt is
  cancelled, the waiter is removed from the queue and, if the mutex
  became unlocked, the next waiter is woken. The previous code used
  `self.waiter_id.take()` which consumed the ID, but the subsequent
  state mutation still needed the waiter_id for cleanup. Now reads
  the ID without taking it.

Mutex deadlock test + semaphore:
  Minor adjustments to test assertions and formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
